message:
feat(conformance): replace stub LabRuntimeTarget with real runtime-backed operations

The conformance test harness previously used placeholder stubs for
spawn(), create_region(), and cancel() — returning dummy
TaskId::new_for_test(0,0) handles with no actual runtime integration.
This meant conformance tests never exercised real task scheduling,
region lifecycle, or cancellation propagation through the Lab runtime.

Replace with a deferred-operation session pattern:

  LabConformanceSession — thread-local queue of pending operations that
  are drained between scheduler steps inside block_on(). Operations
  enqueued via session.enqueue() execute with &mut LabRuntime access,
  bridging the gap between the async conformance API (which only has &Cx)
  and the runtime mutations that require exclusive LabRuntime access.

Key changes:

- TaskHandle/RegionHandle: IDs are now Arc<Mutex<Option<T>>> instead of
  bare values, since the ID isn't known until the deferred op runs. The
  id() accessor returns Option<TaskId>/Option<RegionId> accordingly.

- spawn(): Enqueues a deferred op that calls scope.spawn_registered() on
  the real runtime state, wires up the scheduler, and sends the
  RuntimeTaskHandle back through a oneshot channel. The returned
  TaskHandle awaits registration, then joins the real handle and maps
  JoinError variants to Outcome.

- create_region(): Enqueues creation of a real child region via
  runtime.state.create_child_region(), captures the close_notify, and
  returns a RegionHandle whose Future polls the close notification.

- cancel(): Enqueues request_lab_region_cancel() which propagates
  cancellation through runtime.state and schedules cancel tasks.

- block_on(): Now runs a drain-step loop instead of a single
  run_until_quiescent(), interleaving session.drain() with
  runtime.step_for_test() until both the runtime is quiescent and no
  pending ops remain.

- cancel() signature changed from (cx, region_id, reason) to
  (cx, &RegionHandle, reason) to allow access to the deferred region ID.

Tests added:
- lab_runtime_target_spawn_registers_real_task_handle: verifies spawned
  task gets a real (non-stub) TaskId and produces correct outcome
- lab_runtime_target_create_region_and_cancel_before_registration_closes_region:
  verifies region creation + cancel + await lifecycle works end-to-end

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
